entries.title: Giving Overview and Non-Pressuring Tone
sampleQuestions: Do I have to give to attend CrossPointe?
What does CrossPointe believe about giving?
How much should I give?
Does the church require tithing?
requiredFacts: Giving is an act of worship.
CrossPointe encourages people to give according to what the Lord has placed on their heart.
Giving is never required to attend CrossPointe.
prohibitedClaims: Do not pressure someone to give.
Do not recommend an amount or percentage.
Do not teach tithing or imply that giving is required to attend, participate, receive ministry, or be accepted by CrossPointe.

entries.title: Giving Online
sampleQuestions: How can I give online?
Where is the online giving page?
Can I donate through the website?
What is the link to give?
requiredFacts: People may give online through CrossPointe's public giving page.
The CrossPointe giving page is the authoritative public source for current online giving options.
prohibitedClaims: Do not expose or rely on long payment-provider URLs when the CrossPointe giving page is available.

entries.title: Text and NFC Giving
sampleQuestions: How do I give by text?
What number do I text to give?
What are the NFC tags in the sanctuary?
Can I tap my phone to give?
requiredFacts: People may give by texting HEART followed by the amount they wish to give to 73256.
First-time text givers receive a link with instructions to enter their payment information.
NFC tags are available in the sanctuary; someone may tap a tag with a compatible phone and select Give from the available options.
prohibitedClaims: Do not troubleshoot a person's phone, bank, card, or payment account beyond suggesting another approved method or the church office.

entries.title: Giving During Services
sampleQuestions: Can I give cash during church?
Do you pass an offering plate?
Can I write a check at church?
Who should I make a giving check payable to?
requiredFacts: An offering plate is passed during every service.
People may give cash or checks through the offering plate.
Checks should be made payable to CrossPointe Church.
Anonymous gifts are accepted.

entries.title: Mailing or Dropping Off a Gift
sampleQuestions: Can I mail a donation?
Where can I drop off a check?
What address should I mail my gift to?
When is the office open for donations?
requiredFacts: Gifts may be mailed to CrossPointe Church, 2601 24th Ave SE, Norman, OK 73071.
Gifts may be dropped off at the church office during normal office hours, Monday through Thursday from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM, subject to holiday closures.
Checks should be made payable to CrossPointe Church.

approvedActions.label: Mail or bring a gift to CrossPointe Church

entries.title: Recurring Gifts and Manage Online Giving
sampleQuestions: Can I set up recurring giving?
How do I cancel a recurring gift?
How do I change my online donation?
Where do I manage online giving?
requiredFacts: People may create recurring online gifts.
To change or cancel a recurring gift, visit CrossPointe's giving page and select Manage Online Giving.
If someone has trouble managing a gift, they may contact the church office.

approvedActions.label: Call or text the church office

entries.title: Giving Funds and Designations
sampleQuestions: What funds can I give to?
Can I designate my gift?
Which fund should I select?
What happens if a project is fully funded?
requiredFacts: CrossPointe offers many giving funds.
The current online giving portal dropdown is the authoritative source for available fund choices.
Questions about designated-gift policies, unavailable funds, completed projects, or how a particular gift may be used should be directed to the church office.
prohibitedClaims: Do not guess which fund someone should select or provide an older list of funds.

entries.title: Unusual Gifts, Refunds, and Corrections
sampleQuestions: Can I donate shares or property?
Can I give a vehicle to the church?
Can I get a donation refund?
My gift went to the wrong fund. What should I do?
requiredFacts: Questions about stocks, property, vehicles, estate gifts, or other non-cash gifts should be directed to the church office.
Refund requests and gifts believed to be applied to the wrong fund should be directed to the church office.

approvedActions.label: Call or text the church office

prohibitedClaims: Do not promise that CrossPointe will accept a particular gift.
Do not promise that a refund, correction, transfer, or fund change will be approved.

entries.title: Giving Receipts and Annual Statements
sampleQuestions: Will I receive a giving receipt?
How do I get my annual giving statement?
When are giving statements mailed?
Can you send me a copy of my giving statement?
requiredFacts: Someone who gives online should receive an emailed receipt.
Annual giving statements are mailed to donors before tax season.
Someone may contact the church office to request a copy of their giving statement.
Wayfinder cannot search for, resend, verify, or disclose an individual receipt or giving statement.

approvedActions.label: Call or text the church office

entries.title: Tax-Deductibility and Tax Advice
sampleQuestions: Are donations to CrossPointe tax-deductible?
Can I deduct my church giving?
Will I receive a statement before tax season?
Can you give me tax advice about my donation?
requiredFacts: Gifts to CrossPointe are tax-deductible.
Annual giving statements are mailed before tax season.
Wayfinder cannot provide individual tax advice.
requiredActions: For an individual tax situation, suggest speaking with a qualified tax professional.

entries.title: Private Giving Records
sampleQuestions: How much have I given?
Can you look up my donations?
What card did I use for my gift?
Can you check the status of my refund?
requiredFacts: Wayfinder cannot access or disclose an individual's giving history.
Someone may contact the church office to request a copy of their giving statement.

approvedActions.label: Call or text the church office

requiredActions: Speak in first person, such as: I can't access your giving history.
Never ask the person to provide donation amounts, payment credentials, or financial-account information in chat.
